sheet_name: แปลนระบบสุขาภิบาลพื้นชั้นล่าง, แบบขยายระบบสุขาภิบาลห้องน้ำ
view_title: แปลนระบบสุขาภิบาลห้องน้ำ (ท่อประปาเดินใต้พื้น)

elements.description: Ø3/4"CW cold-water supply pipe entering top-left of detail, routed down through the wall toward the fixture cluster
Ø2 1/2"W waste pipe from lavatory/fixtures, routed toward the floor-drain cluster
Ø4"S soil pipe from water closet (WC symbol) toward the main stack
Ø2"FD floor drain (two callouts of 'Ø2"FD' visible), with FCO (floor cleanout) marked adjacent to each
Ø2"V+AVC vent pipe with vent cap at bottom-left of the detail
second Ø3/4"CW label near the bottom of the detail, likely marking the stop-valve branch into the WC/basin fixtures

sibling_files: บ้าน_เล็ก_1ชั้น_02_หน้า39_floor_plan.json
warnings: This is a small-scale (1:50) schematic detail plan of underfloor bathroom piping, not on the main building grid -- grid_ref_is_free_text_not_grid_notation is correctly true here (unlike the sibling main floor plan on this same page, which IS on the real structural grid).

This detail is near-identical in content and pipe-size callouts (Ø3/4"CW, Ø2 1/2"W, Ø4"S, Ø2"FD, Ø2"V+AVC) to house #1's equivalent bathroom-plan inset on page29 -- both houses appear to reuse the same standard underfloor-bathroom-piping detail, adjusted only for this room's own overall dimensions (2.70 x 1.80 m here vs 2.45 x 1.80 m on house #1).

Exact fixture layout (WC/lavatory positions relative to walls) and precise routing paths between fixtures remain only partially legible at this resolution -- positions are best-effort, not measured coordinates.
